title: SOP for populating EBI submission  templates (ENA)
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"e9n6g","text":"Guidance on how to populate the extended PHA4GE metadata package for SARS-CoV-2 submissions, maximizing interoperability for covid-19 surveillance. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Guidance for populating the three templates for SARS-CoV-2 submission to EBI. This protocol helps to describe the fields needed to populate the ENA virus pathogen checklist, however, the primary PHA4GE guidance should be followed to ensure the controlled vocabularies and ontology terms are used to populate these fields. Link to PHA4GE SARS-CoV-2 metadata specification: https://github.com/pha4ge/SARS-CoV-2-Contextual-Data-Specification Link to PHA4GE SARS-CoV-2 EBI submission protocol: ENA, BioSample, and BioProject: https://www.protocols.io/private/BD4C35AC52C942B2D927E662ABC3D195 Link to PHA4GE SARS-CoV2 EBI assembly submission protocol https://www.protocols.io/private/39BBC8E9B6F911EAA1530A58A9FEAC2A

PHA4GE ENA virus pathogen reporting standard checklist for sample metadata: ENA submission spreadsheets are not tables with fields name in the first row, and specfics about each sample in subsequent rows. Instead the spreadsheet requires particular headers (denoted with "#"). e.g %s %s As described in the PHA4GE SARS-CoV-2 EBI submission protocol , there will be an oppotunity to download a template spreadsheet from ENA directly. We recommend that you add aditional fields as per the PHA4GE guidelines and keep a blank copy of this spreadsheet for subsequent submissions.

Populate ENA run metadata table: PRO TIPS: If you have sequences to submit that have drastically different metadata, create a separate submission + metadata table for each case. Entering fastq filenames in the spreadsheet : On a Mac, you can directly copy the file names from the folder into a spreadsheet. This is not possible on a PC using copy and paste but can be done with some command-line operation. Finally, it is important to develop a QA/QC step to make sure the files are associated with the correct sample name. For example, use a left function in excel to strip of the appended text in the file name and then use the exact match to make sure the name matches the sample name.

As described in the PHA4GE SARS-CoV-2 EBI submission protocol , there will be an oppotunity to download a template spreadsheet from ENA directly. There is a description of these fields in the table below. %s %s

Guidlines to populate the sample metadata sheet These fields are required for all ENA submission. They do not appear as part of the checklist. The description and guidance is tabulated below. Description of required fields %s %s Additional Required Fields for Pathogens (not in template, but appear in submission process)ENA Virus Checklist FieldDefinitionsample_namevirus identifierThe user-provided name of the sample.tax_idN/AThe NCBITaxon identifier for the organism being sequenced.scientific_nameN/AThe taxonomic name of the organism.common_nameN/AThe common name of the organism.sample_descriptionN/AFree text description of the sample.instrument_model N/AName of the sequencing instrument.library_source N/AMolecule type used to make the library.library_selection N/ALibrary capture method.library_strategy N/AOverall sequencing strategy or approach.library_layout N/ASingle or paired.file_name N/AInclude ALL of the files resulting from this library. **Add additional fields if there are more than two files (e.g. Filename3) Guidance of required fields %s %s Additional Required Fields for Pathogens (not in template, but appear in submission process)ENA Virus Checklist FieldDefinitionENA Requirement StatusPHA4GE FieldPHA4GE Guidancesample_namevirus identifierThe user-provided name of the sample.mandatoryspecimen collector sample IDThis field can be populated by the PHA4GE field "specimen collector sample ID".tax_idN/AThe NCBITaxon identifier for the organism being sequenced.mandatoryN/AUse "2697049" as the tad_id for SARS-CoV-2.scientific_nameN/AThe taxonomic name of the organism.mandatoryorganismThis field can be populated by the PHA4GE field "organism". Provide the full name "Severe acute respiratory syndrome coronavirus 2".common_nameN/AThe common name of the organism.mandatoryN/AThis field can be populated by the PHA4GE field "host (common name)". Provide "Sars-CoV-2".sample_descriptionN/AFree text description of the sample.mandatoryN/Ainstrument_model N/AName of the sequencing instrument.mandatoryN/ASee ENA SRA pick list. (e.g. Illumina MiSeq, iSeq 100, GridION, MinION, PacBio Sequel II)library_source N/AMolecule type used to make the library.mandatoryN/ASee ENA SRA pick list. (e.g. viral RNA, metagenomic)library_selection N/ALibrary capture method.mandatoryN/ASee ENA SRA pick list. (e.g. random, PCR)library_strategy N/AOverall sequencing strategy or approach.mandatoryN/ASee ENA SRA pick list. (e.g. WGS, RNA-Seq, Amplicon)library_layout N/ASingle or paired.mandatoryN/ASee ENA SRA pick list. (single, paired)file_name N/AInclude ALL of the files resulting from this library. **Add additional fields if there are more than two files (e.g. Filename3)mandatoryr1 fastq filenameThis field can be populated by the PHA4GE field "r1 fastq filename". For SARSCov2 submission, ENA ask that the metadata comply with Checklist ERC000033: https://www.ebi.ac.uk/ena/browser/view/ERC000033 . These fields below are drawn from the ENA Checklist, with description and mapping to the respective PHA4GE field should help you transfer your metadata from the PHA4GE table to the something acceptable for ENA submission. %s %s ENA Virus Checklist FieldENA DefinitionENA Requirement StatusPHA4GE Fieldsubject exposure Exposure of the subject to infected human or animals, such as poultry, wild bird or swine. If multiple exposures are applicable, please state them separated by semicolon. Example: poultry; wild birdoptionalexposure eventsubject exposure durationDuration of the exposure of the subject to an infected human or animal. If multiple exposures are applicable, please state their duration in the same order in which you reported the exposure in the field 'subject exposure'. Example: 1 day; 0.33 daysoptionalN/Atype exposureSetting within which the subject is exposed to animals, such as farm, slaughterhouse, food preparation. If multiple exposures are applicable, please state their type in the same order in which you reported the exposure in the field 'subject exposure'. Example: backyard flock; confined animal feeding operationoptionalexposure eventpersonal protective equipmentUse of personal protective equipment, such as gloves, gowns, during any type of exposure. Example: maskoptionalN/AhospitalisationWas the subject confined to a hospital as a result of virus infection or problems occurring secondary to virus infection?optionalspecified as value under host health statusillness durationThe number of days the illness lasted. Example: 4optionalN/Aillness symptomsThe symptoms that have been reported in relation to the illness, such as cough, diarrhea, fever, headache, malaise, myalgia, nausea, runny_nose, shortness_of_breath, sore_throat. If multiple exposures are applicabloptional signs and symptomscollection dateThe date of sampling, either as an instance (single point in time) or interval. In case no exact time is available, the date/time can be right truncated i.e. all of these are valid ISO8601 compliant times: 2008-01-23T19:23:10+00:00; 2008-01-23T19:23:10; 2008-01-23; 2008-01; 2008.recommendedsample collection dategeographic location (country and/or sea)The geographical origin of the sample as defined by the country or sea. Country or sea names should be chosen from the INSDC country list (http://insdc.org/country.html).mandatorygeo_loc (country)geographic location (latitude)The geographical origin of the sample as defined by latitude and longitude. The values should be reported in decimal degrees and in WGS84 systemrecommendedgeo_loc latitudegeographic location (longitude)The geographical origin of the sample as defined by latitude and longitude. The values should be reported in decimal degrees and in WGS84 systemrecommendedgeo_loc longitudegeographic location (region and locality)The geographical origin of the sample as defined by the specific region name followed by the locality name.recommendedgeo_loc (state/province/region)sample capture statusReason for the sample collection.recommendedpurpose of samplinghost disease outcomeDisease outcome in the host.recommendedhost disease outcomehost common namecommon name of the host, e.g. humanmandatoryhost (common name)host subject ida unique identifier by which each subject can be referred to, de-identified, e.g. #131mandatoryhost subject IDhost ageage of host at the time of sampling; relevant scale depends on species and study, e.g. could be seconds for amoebae or centuries for treesrecommendedhost agehost health stateHealth status of the host at the time of sample collection.mandatoryhost health statehost sexGender or sex of the host.mandatoryhost genderhost scientific nameScientific name of the natural (as opposed to laboratory) host to the organism from which sample was obtained.mandatoryhost (scientific name)virus identifierUnique laboratory identifier assigned to the virus by the investigator. Strain name is not sufficient since it might not be unique due to various passsages of the same virus. Format: up to 50 alphanumeric charactersrecommendedspecimen collector sample IDcollector nameName of the person who collected the specimen. Example: John SmithmandatoryN/Acollecting institutionName of the institution to which the person collecting the specimen belongs. Format: Institute Name, Institute Addressmandatorysample collected byreceipt dateDate on which the sample was received. Format:YYYY-MM-DD. Please provide the highest precision possible. If the sample was received by the institution and not collected, the 'receipt date' must be provided instead. Either the 'collection date' or 'receipt date' must be provided. If available, provide both dates.recommendedreceived datesample storage conditionsConditions at which sample was stored, usually storage temperature, duration and locationoptionalN/Adefinition for seropositive sampleThe cut off value used by an investigatior in determining that a sample was seropositive.recommendedN/Aserotype (required for a seropositive sample)Serological variety of a species characterised by its antigenic properties. For Influenza, HA subtype should be the letter H followed by a number between 1-16 unless novel subtype is identified and the NA subtype should be the letter N followed by a number between 1-9 unless novel subtype is identified. If only one of the subtypes have been tested then use the format H5Nx or HxN1. Example: H1N1recommendedN/Aisolateindividual isolate from which the sample was obtainedmandatoryisolatestrainName of the strain from which the sample was obtained.optionalN/Ahost habitatNatural habitat of the avian or mammalian host.recommendedN/Aisolation source host-associatedName of host tissue or organ sampled for analysis. Example: tracheal tissuerecommendedanatomical material; anatomical part; body producthost descriptionOther descriptive information relating to the host.optionalN/AgravidityWhether or not the subject is gravid. If so, report date due or date post-conception and specify which of these two dates is being reported.optionalN/Ahost behaviourNatural behaviour of the host.recommendedN/Aisolation source non-host-associatedDescribes the physical, environmental and/or local geographical source of the biological sample from which the sample was derived. Example: soilrecommendedenvironmental site; environmental material %s %s ENA Virus Checklist FieldENA DefinitionENA Requirement StatusPHA4GE FieldPHA4GE Guidancesubject exposure Exposure of the subject to infected human or animals, such as poultry, wild bird or swine. If multiple exposures are applicable, please state them separated by semicolon. Example: poultry; wild birdoptionalexposure eventThis field can be populated by the PHA4GE field "exposure event". Caution: this may be sensistive information. Consult the data steward before sharing. If the information is unknown, not applicable, or can not be shared, leave blank or provide a null value.subject exposure durationDuration of the exposure of the subject to an infected human or animal. If multiple exposures are applicable, please state their duration in the same order in which you reported the exposure in the field 'subject exposure'. Example: 1 day; 0.33 daysoptionalN/AIf the information is unknown, not applicable, or not available, leave blank or provide a null value.type exposureSetting within which the subject is exposed to animals, such as farm, slaughterhouse, food preparation. If multiple exposures are applicable, please state their type in the same order in which you reported the exposure in the field 'subject exposure'. Example: backyard flock; confined animal feeding operationoptionalexposure eventThe PHA4GE field "exposure event" describes subject exposures and settings. Exposure information captured in the PHA4GE specification can be provided either in the ENA "subject exposure" or the "type exposure" field.personal protective equipmentUse of personal protective equipment, such as gloves, gowns, during any type of exposure. Example: maskoptionalN/AIf the information is unknown, not applicable, or not available, leave blank or provide a null value.hospitalisationWas the subject confined to a hospital as a result of virus infection or problems occurring secondary to virus infection?optionalspecified as value under host health statusThis information can be found as values in the "host health status" field in the PHA4GE specification e.g. Hospitalized, Hospitalized (ICU), Hospitalized (Non-ICU). The ENA "hospitalisation" field requires yes/no values. Provide yes/no values for this field if submitting to ENA. If the information is unknown, or can not be shared, leave blank or provide a null value. illness durationThe number of days the illness lasted. Example: 4optionalN/AIf the information is unknown, not applicable, or not available, leave blank or provide a null value.illness symptomsThe symptoms that have been reported in relation to the illness, such as cough, diarrhea, fever, headache, malaise, myalgia, nausea, runny_nose, shortness_of_breath, sore_throat. If multiple exposures are applicabloptional signs and symptomsThis field can be populated by the PHA4GE field "signs and symptoms". If the information is unknown, not applicable, or not available, leave blank or provide a null value.collection dateThe date of sampling, either as an instance (single point in time) or interval. In case no exact time is available, the date/time can be right truncated i.e. all of these are valid ISO8601 compliant times: 2008-01-23T19:23:10+00:00; 2008-01-23T19:23:10; 2008-01-23; 2008-01; 2008.recommendedsample collection dateThis field can be populated by the PHA4GE field "sample collection date". Caution: the sample collection date may be considered public health identifiable information. Consult the data steward before sharing. Acceptable formats are YYYY-MM-DD, YYYY-MM, or YYYY.geographic location (country and/or sea)The geographical origin of the sample as defined by the country or sea. Country or sea names should be chosen from the INSDC country list (http://insdc.org/country.html).mandatorygeo_loc (country)This field can be populated by the PHA4GE field "geo_loc name (country)". The values in the PHA4GE pick list are derived from the INSDC country list.geographic location (latitude)The geographical origin of the sample as defined by latitude and longitude. The values should be reported in decimal degrees and in WGS84 systemrecommendedgeo_loc latitudeThis field can be populated by the PHA4GE field "geo_loc latitude". Caution: this is likely sensitive information. Consult the data steward before sharing. Do not provide latitude of the institution, nor the centre of the city/region where the sample was collected as this falsely implicates an existing location. If the information is unknown or can not be shared, leave blank or provide a null value. geographic location (longitude)The geographical origin of the sample as defined by latitude and longitude. The values should be reported in decimal degrees and in WGS84 systemrecommendedgeo_loc longitudeThis field can be populated by the PHA4GE field "geo_loc longitude". Caution: this is likely sensitive information. Consult the data steward before sharing. Do not provide longitude of the institution, nor the centre of the city/region where the sample was collected as this falsely implicates an existing location. If the information is unknown or can not be shared, leave blank or provide a null value.geographic location (region and locality)The geographical origin of the sample as defined by the specific region name followed by the locality name.recommendedgeo_loc (state/province/region)This field can be populated by the PHA4GE field "geo_loc (state/province/region". Caution: this maye be sensitive information depending on the number of cases in this geographic area. Consult the data steward before sharing. If the information is unknown or can not be shared, leave blank or provide a null value.sample capture statusReason for the sample collection.recommendedpurpose of samplingWhile the meanings of ENA's "sample capture status" and PHA4GE's "purpose of sampling" fields overlap in meaning, ENA provides a specific pick list of terms for populating the field. Use the ENA pick list if submitting to ENA. If the information is unknown or can not be shared, leave blank or provide a null value.host disease outcomeDisease outcome in the host.recommendedhost disease outcomeWhile the meanings of ENA's "host disease outcome" and PHA4GE's "host disease outcome" fields overlap in meaning, ENA provides a specific pick list of terms for populating the field. Use the ENA pick list if submitting to ENA. If the information is unknown or can not be shared, leave blank or provide a null value.host common namecommon name of the host, e.g. humanmandatoryhost (common name)This field can be populated by the PHA4GE field "host (common name)".host subject ida unique identifier by which each subject can be referred to, de-identified, e.g. #131mandatoryhost subject IDThis field can be populated by the PHA4GE field "host subject ID". Caution: the host subject ID may be considered public health identifiable information. Consult the data steward before sharing. If unknown or considered identifiable, provide an alternative ID or a null value.host ageage of host at the time of sampling; relevant scale depends on species and study, e.g. could be seconds for amoebae or centuries for treesrecommendedhost ageThis field can be populated by the PHA4GE field "host age". Caution: the host age may be considered public health identifiable information. Consult the data steward before sharing. If the information is unknown or can not be shared, leave blank or provide a null value.host health stateHealth status of the host at the time of sample collection.mandatoryhost health stateWhile the meanings of the ENA and PHA4GE "host health state" fields overlap, ENA requires certain values in this field. If known, provide "diseased" or "healthy". If the information is unknown or can not be shared, provide a null value.host sexGender or sex of the host.mandatoryhost genderWhile the meanings of ENA's "host sex" and PHA4GE's "host gender" fields overlap in meaning, ENA provides a specific pick list of terms for populating the field. Use the ENA pick list if submitting to ENA. Caution: the host gender may be considered public health identifiable information. Consult your data steward before sharing. If the information is unknown or can not be shared, provide a null value.host scientific nameScientific name of the natural (as opposed to laboratory) host to the organism from which sample was obtained.mandatoryhost (scientific name)This field can be populated by the PHA4GE field "host (scientific name)".virus identifierUnique laboratory identifier assigned to the virus by the investigator. Strain name is not sufficient since it might not be unique due to various passsages of the same virus. Format: up to 50 alphanumeric charactersrecommendedspecimen collector sample IDThis field can be populated by the PHA4GE field "specimen collector sample ID". Caution: the sample ID may be considered sensitive information. Consult the data steward. You may need to provide an alternative ID.collector nameName of the person who collected the specimen. Example: John SmithmandatoryN/AProvide the name of the person who collected the sample. If the information is unknown or can not be shared, provide a null value.collecting institutionName of the institution to which the person collecting the specimen belongs. Format: Institute Name, Institute Addressmandatorysample collected byThis field can be populated by the PHA4GE field "sample collected by". Caution: if the name of the lab reveals geographic information, this may be considered public health identifiable information. Consult the data steward before sharing. If information ca not be shared, provide a null value.receipt dateDate on which the sample was received. Format:YYYY-MM-DD. Please provide the highest precision possible. If the sample was received by the institution and not collected, the 'receipt date' must be provided instead. Either the 'collection date' or 'receipt date' must be provided. If available, provide both dates.recommendedreceived dateThis field can be populated by the PHA4GE field "received date". If the information is unknown or can not be shared, leave blank or provide a null value.sample storage conditionsConditions at which sample was stored, usually storage temperature, duration and locationoptionalN/AIf the information is unknown, not applicable, or not available, leave blank or provide a null value.definition for seropositive sampleThe cut off value used by an investigatior in determining that a sample was seropositive.recommendedN/AIf the information is unknown, not applicable, or not available, leave blank or provide a null value.serotype (required for a seropositive sample)Serological variety of a species characterised by its antigenic properties. For Influenza, HA subtype should be the letter H followed by a number between 1-16 unless novel subtype is identified and the NA subtype should be the letter N followed by a number between 1-9 unless novel subtype is identified. If only one of the subtypes have been tested then use the format H5Nx or HxN1. Example: H1N1recommendedN/AIf the information is unknown, not applicable, or not available, leave blank or provide a null value.isolateindividual isolate from which the sample was obtainedmandatoryisolateThis field can be populated by the PHA4GE field "isolate". strainName of the strain from which the sample was obtained.optionalN/AIf the information is unknown, not applicable, or not available, leave blank or provide a null value.host habitatNatural habitat of the avian or mammalian host.recommendedN/AIf the information is unknown, not applicable, or not available, leave blank or provide a null value.isolation source host-associatedName of host tissue or organ sampled for analysis. Example: tracheal tissuerecommendedanatomical material; anatomical part; body productThis field can be populated by the PHA4GE fields "anatomical material", "anatomical part" and "body product". If the information is unknown, not applicable, or can not be shared, leave blank or provide a null value.host descriptionOther descriptive information relating to the host.optionalN/ACan be left blank.gravidityWhether or not the subject is gravid. If so, report date due or date post-conception and specify which of these two dates is being reported.optionalN/AIf the information is unknown, not applicable, or not available, leave blank or provide a null value.host behaviourNatural behaviour of the host.recommendedN/AIf the information is unknown, not applicable, or not available, leave blank or provide a null value.isolation source non-host-associatedDescribes the physical, environmental and/or local geographical source of the biological sample from which the sample was derived. Example: soilrecommendedenvironmental site; environmental materialThis field can be populated by the PHA4GE fields "environmental site" and "environmental material". If the information is unknown, not applicable, or can not be shared, leave blank or provide a null value. There is extended guideance available at the PHA4GE SARS-CoV-2 metadata specification: https://github.com/pha4ge/SARS-CoV-2-Contextual-Data-Specification
creator: Nabil-Fareed Alikhan
